roles.title: Choose Your Role
roles.subtitle: Try different careers. Discover what fits you.

drone_programmer.name: Drone Programmer
drone_programmer.description: Write code to control drones in real Saudi Arabia scenarios

robot_constructor.name: Robot Constructor
robot_constructor.description: Build and optimize robots for industrial missions

entrepreneur.name: Tech Entrepreneur
entrepreneur.description: Launch a tech startup and make strategic decisions

specialMissions.title: Special Missions
specialMissions.desc: Exclusive missions from industry partners and top universities. Complete them to earn special rewards and recognition.

drone.m1_desc: Program a drone to deliver medical supplies to an oil platform. Avoid obstacles.
drone.m2_desc: Optimize a drone delivery route across Riyadh to minimize time and battery use.

m1.title: First Flight
m1.description: Deliver construction materials to the BRICS City building site. Assemble a program to fly your drone from start to target.
m1.hint1: The drone needs to go right and up. Start with takeoff.
m1.success: The drone reached its destination! Materials delivered successfully.
m1.fail: The drone didn't reach the target. Try adjusting your program.

step1.title: Welcome, Drone Programmer!
step1.description: You've been recruited to help build BRICS City. Your job: program delivery drones.

step2.title: How Drone Programming Works
step2.description: You'll assemble command blocks into a program. The drone follows your commands step by step.

step3.title: Your Toolkit
step3.description: Drag blocks from the palette to build your program. Try takeoff, forward, turn, and land.

step4.title: How You Score
step4.description: Reach the target to pass. Use fewer blocks and finish faster for a higher score. Up to 3 stars!

step5.title: Ready for Takeoff!
step5.description: Your first mission: fly from the bottom-left corner to the top-right. Good luck, pilot!

m2.title: Mapping
m2.description: Photograph the territory by flying over all marked points. Use repeat blocks to be efficient.
m2.hint1: You need to visit all blue squares. Can you find a pattern?
m2.hint2: The points form a zigzag. Use repeat(N) to avoid duplicating commands.
m2.hint3: Optimal: takeoff, repeat(2) '{' forward(2), turnRight, forward(2), turnLeft '}', forward(2), land — 8 blocks total.

m3.title: Night Watch
m3.description: Program a patrol drone for the night construction site. Cover 80%+ of the territory.
m3.hint1: A good patrol visits every area. Think about a snake-like pattern.
m3.hint2: Try: takeoff, repeat(2) '{' forward(4), turnRight, forward(1), turnRight, forward(4), turnLeft, forward(1), turnLeft '}', land
m3.hint3: Optimal solution uses 7 blocks: takeoff + repeat(2) containing forward(4), turnRight, forward(1), turnRight + land. This creates a snake pattern covering all cells.

m4.title: Storm Delivery
m4.description: Deliver medicine through a sandstorm. Wind pushes the drone 1 cell after each move. Compensate to reach the target!
m4.hint1: Wind cycles through N, E, S, W. After your first forward, wind blows North.
m4.hint2: If wind blows North, your drone moves 1 cell up after your move. Counter by moving South extra.
m4.hint3: Plan each move + wind effect: forward(1) + wind(N) = net 2 cells if going North. Use turns to compensate!

m5.title: Drone Swarm
m5.description: Write JavaScript to control 3 drones installing solar panels at 3 locations.
m5.hint1: You have drones[0], drones[1], drones[2] and targets[0], targets[1], targets[2].
m5.hint2: Use drones[i].flyTo(targets[i]) to send each drone to its target, then drones[i].install("solar_panel").
m5.hint3: Wrap it in a loop: for (let i = 0; i < 3; i++) '{' drones[i].flyTo(targets[i]); drones[i].install("solar_panel"); '}'

m6.title: Sensor Maze
m6.description: Navigate a maze using sensor API. Write a wall-following algorithm to reach the exit.
m6.hint1: Use drone.seeWall("front") to check if there's a wall ahead before moving.
m6.hint2: The right-hand rule: always keep your right hand on the wall. Turn right when possible, go straight when you can't.
m6.hint3: while (!drone.atExit()) '{' if (!drone.seeWall("right")) '{' drone.turnRight(); drone.forward(1); '}' else if (!drone.seeWall("front")) '{' drone.forward(1); '}' else '{' drone.turnLeft(); '}' '}'

m7.title: Search & Rescue
m7.description: Write a search algorithm to find survivors using thermal scanning on a disaster grid.
m7.hint1: Use nested for loops: for (let y = 0; y < 10; y++) '{' for (let x = 0; x < 10; x++) '{' ... '}' '}'
m7.hint2: Fly to each cell, scan heat. If scanHeat() returns 100, drop a marker — that's a survivor!
m7.hint3: for (let y = 0; y < 10; y++) '{' for (let x = 0; x < 10; x++) '{' drone.flyTo(x, y); let heat = drone.scanHeat(); if (heat === 100) '{' drone.dropMarker("red"); '}' '}' '}'

m8.title: Blind Navigation
m8.description: Navigate to a target with no GPS. Use camera and landmarks to find your way through fog.
m8.hint1: Start by scanning for landmarks with drone.camera.scan(). It shows nearby towers.
m8.hint2: Use drone.rotate(angle) to point toward the target area, then drone.forward(n) to move.
m8.hint3: A simple approach: rotate toward (7,7) and move forward step by step. Use angleTo to calculate direction.

m9.title: Air Convoy
m9.description: Manage 5 delivery drones to fulfill 10 orders. Route each drone efficiently.
m9.hint1: Each order has a pickup and dropoff location. Send a drone to pickup, grab(), fly to dropoff, drop().
m9.hint2: Use a for loop: for (let i = 0; i < 10; i++) to process each order with fleet[i % 5].
m9.hint3: for (let i = 0; i < 10; i++) '{' let d = fleet[i % 5]; d.flyTo(orders[i].pickup.x, orders[i].pickup.y); d.grab(); d.flyTo(orders[i].dropoff.x, orders[i].dropoff.y); d.drop(); '}'

m10.title: Smart City System
m10.description: Handle mixed city events with 8 drones. Prioritize emergencies over deliveries and patrols.
m10.hint1: Sort events by priority first: events.sort((a, b) => b.priority - a.priority)
m10.hint2: Use findClosest(fleet, event.location) to assign the nearest free drone to each event.
m10.hint3: events.sort((a, b) => b.priority - a.priority); for (let i = 0; i < events.length; i++) '{' let d = findClosest(fleet, events[i].location); d.flyTo(events[i].location.x, events[i].location.y); d.handleEvent(events[i].id); '}'

robot.m1_desc: Build a robot to work on an oil pipeline assembly line within a tight budget.
robot.m2_desc: Design a robot for search-and-rescue operations in a desert environment.

m1.title: First Helper
m1.description: Build your first robot to lift a 10kg box. Choose the right parts within budget.
m1.hint1: The issue is in the motor or chassis. Your robot needs enough strength to lift the box.
m1.hint2: Use Medium Chassis + Standard Motor + Basic Gripper + Medium Wheels. Strength >= 5 is needed.
m1.success: Your robot successfully lifted the box! Great engineering.
m1.fail: The robot couldn't lift the box. Check your parts and try again.

step1.title: Welcome, Robot Constructor!
step1.description: You've been recruited to build robots for BRICS City. Each robot needs the right parts for the job.

step2.title: How Robot Building Works
step2.description: Drag parts from the catalog into robot slots. Each part has stats: strength, precision, speed, weight, and cost.

step3.title: Your Workshop
step3.description: Fill all 5 slots: chassis, arms, wheels, motor, and battery. Watch your budget and weight limits!

step4.title: How You Score
step4.description: Fill all slots correctly, pass the test, stay under budget, and build smoothly. Up to 3 stars!

step5.title: Time to Build!
step5.description: Your first task: build a robot that can lift a 10kg box. Budget: 80 coins. Let's go!

m1.partsCatalogHint: Drag parts to the robot blueprint slots on the left
m1.animIdle: Press "Test Robot" to see the animation
m1.missionDesc: Assemble a robot that can lift a 10kg box. Drag parts onto the blueprint.

m2.title: Wheels or Legs?
m2.description: Choose the right locomotion system for rough terrain.
m2.hint1: Test each chassis on all three surfaces before deciding.
m2.hint2: Only one chassis type can handle sand, rocks, AND stairs.
m2.story: Your robot needs to work on rough terrain with sand, rocks, and stairs. You must choose the right locomotion system to handle all conditions.
m2.requirement: Task: Robot must handle ALL THREE surfaces (sand, rocks, stairs).
m2.animIdle: Select a chassis and surface, then press "Test"

m3.title: Balance Master
m3.description: Balance fragile cargo on a robot crossing a narrow bridge.
m3.hint1: Heavy items should be near the center. Distribute weight evenly left and right.
m3.hint2: Try placing the 6kg panel and 5kg panel on opposite sides.
m3.story: Your robot must carry fragile solar panels across a narrow bridge. If the load is unbalanced, the robot will wobble and fall off!
m3.requirement: Task: Place all cargo items and balance the load so the robot can cross safely.
m3.cargoHint: Drag cargo items to the placement zones on the robot
m3.balanceOk: Slightly off-center. Robot will wobble but can cross.
m3.balanceDanger: Too unbalanced! Robot will fall off the bridge!
m3.animIdle: Place cargo and press "Cross Bridge" to test

m4.title: Gear Ratio
m4.description: Choose the right gear ratio and motor to climb a steep quarry slope with heavy cargo.
m4.hint1: Force = Torque x Gear Ratio. You need at least 500N to climb the slope. Try increasing the gear ratio.
m4.hint2: A gear ratio between 4:1 and 6:1 with the medium motor gives enough force without overheating.
m4.story: Your robot must carry a 50kg load up a steep 30-degree slope in a quarry. Choose the right motor and gear ratio so the robot has enough force to climb without overheating.
m4.requirement: Task: Select a motor and gear ratio so the robot climbs the slope successfully.
m4.resultSlides: Not enough force! Try a higher gear ratio or bigger motor.
m4.resultOverheats: Speed too low, motor burns out! Try a lower gear ratio.
m4.animIdle: Configure motor and gear ratio, then press "Test Climb"

m5.title: Robot Welder
m5.description: Configure a welding arm to reach all weld points on a bridge blueprint.
m5.hint1: The arm's total reach is the sum of all 3 segments. Check the distance to each weld point on the blueprint.
m5.hint2: For best weld quality, set high precision and low speed. The farthest point (W5) is about 445cm away.
m5.story: A bridge is being built and needs welding at 5 critical joints. Configure the robot's 3-segment welding arm to reach all weld points, then set precision and speed for quality welds.
m5.requirement: Task: Adjust arm segments to reach all 5 weld points, then set precision and speed.
m5.qualityHint: High precision + low speed = best welds. But slower means higher cost.

m6.title: Power Budget
m6.description: Plan a robot's power system to last a full 8-hour workday on a solar farm.
m6.hint1: Total energy = power consumption (watts) x hours. Make sure your battery + solar covers 8 hours.
m6.hint2: The solar panel generates 100Wh per hour for 6 hours (600Wh total). A medium battery (500Wh) plus solar might be enough if you disable non-essential components.
m6.story: Your robot must work 8 hours straight on a solar farm without recharging. Choose which components to enable, pick a battery, and optionally add a solar panel. Plan carefully so the energy lasts!
m6.requirement: Task: Configure components and power supply so the robot runs for a full 8-hour shift.

m7.title: Underwater Explorer
m7.description: Design an underwater robot for pipeline inspection.
m7.hint1: The hull must withstand pressure at 500m depth. Pressure = depth x 1000 x 9.8. Choose a material strong enough and boost it with a good hull shape.
m7.hint2: For neutral buoyancy, the displaced water mass must equal the robot mass (ratio close to 1.0). Titanium hull + standard seals + all 4 thrusters is a solid combination within budget.
m7.story: BRICS City needs an underwater robot to inspect deep-sea pipelines at 500 meters depth. Design a robot that can withstand extreme pressure, achieve neutral buoyancy, and maneuver with precision.
m7.requirement: Task: Design all 4 systems (hull, buoyancy, thrusters, seals) within a $150 budget. The robot must reach 500m depth without leaking, sinking, or getting crushed.
m7.archimedesHint: Archimedes principle: buoyant force = weight of displaced water. For neutral buoyancy, robot mass must equal displaced water mass.
m7.tooHeavy: Too heavy - robot will sink uncontrolled
m7.tooLight: Too light - robot will float back up
m7.thrustersHint: Place all 4 thrusters (top, bottom, left, right) for full maneuverability. Each thruster costs $15.
m7.needAllThrusters: All 4 thrusters required for stable control
m7.sealsHint: Gasket seals must withstand the same pressure as the hull. Higher quality = higher pressure rating.

m8.title: Assembly Line
m8.description: Design a multi-robot assembly line for solar panels.
m8.hint1: The bottleneck (slowest station) determines your throughput. Try to balance station speeds so no single station is much slower than the others.
m8.hint2: Welding is the slowest station (90s base time). Set it to speed 7-8, but watch the error rate. Cutting and bending can go faster. Keep inspection slow for quality.
m8.story: The solar panel factory needs an automated assembly line with 4 robot stations. Each panel must be cut, bent, welded, and inspected. Optimize the line to produce 5+ panels per hour with minimal defects.
m8.requirement: Task: Arrange stations, set speeds, and balance throughput vs. quality. Target: 5+ panels/hour with low defect rate.
m8.dragHint: Drag stations to reorder. Adjust speed sliders for each.

m9.title: Stress Test
m9.description: Build a robot that survives extreme heat, sand, and vibration.
m9.hint1: For the heat test: motor generates 30W, ambient is 55C, electronics fail at 70C. You need a heatsink that dissipates at least 15W to stay under 70C.
m9.hint2: Cheapest passing combo: small heatsink ($10) + IP65 ($15) + rubber dampers ($10) = $35 total, well within the $80 budget.
m9.story: Before deployment in the desert, your robot must pass 3 harsh environment tests: extreme heat (55C), a sandstorm, and heavy vibration. Choose the right protection for each challenge.
m9.requirement: Task: Select protection components for heat, dust, and vibration within an $80 budget. All 3 tests must pass.
m9.heatHint: Motor generates 30W heat. Ambient temperature: 55C. Electronics fail at 70C.
m9.dustHint: Sandstorm test - seal rating must be IP65 or higher.
m9.vibrationHint: Vibration test - dampers must reduce vibration by 50%+.

m10.title: Robot Factory
m10.description: Design a complete robot manufacturing facility.
m10.hint1: Each zone needs a charging station within range (the yellow circle). Place stations at intersections of zones to cover multiple areas with fewer stations.
m10.hint2: Select 5 diverse robot types to cover different city needs. Place 3-4 charging stations strategically at zone borders to maximize coverage while staying under $500.
m10.story: BRICS City is ready for its robotic infrastructure! Design the complete system: choose robot types for different city zones, place charging stations, and launch a 12-month simulation to verify coverage.
m10.requirement: Task: Select 5 robots from the catalog, place charging stations on the map, and achieve 100% zone coverage within a $500 budget.

entrepreneur.m1_desc: Launch an educational technology startup for Saudi schools. Choose your team and strategy.
entrepreneur.m2_desc: Your startup has traction. Decide whether to scale locally or expand to BRICS+ markets.

m1.title: The Idea
m1.description: Walk the streets of BRICS City, talk to people, discover their problems, and find your startup idea.
m1.hint1: Costs are growing faster than your understanding. Talk to more people and look for patterns.
m1.hint2: Group similar problems together. The biggest group (4+ people with the same issue) is your best startup idea.
m1.success: Great market research! You found a real problem that many people share.
m1.fail: Your grouping wasn't quite right. Try talking to more people and look for common themes.

step1.title: Welcome, Entrepreneur!
step1.description: You've arrived in BRICS City to start a tech company. But first, you need to find a real problem to solve.

step2.title: How Market Research Works
step2.description: Walk the streets and click on people to hear their problems. Each person has a different story.

step3.title: Your Tools
step3.description: Problems appear as sticky notes. Drag them into columns to group by theme. You can create and rename columns.

step4.title: How You Score
step4.description: Group problems correctly, talk to many people, and work quickly. The biggest problem group = your startup idea!

step5.title: Hit the Streets!
step5.description: 12 people are waiting with their problems. Find the pattern, pick your idea, and launch your startup!

ahmed.name: Ahmed
ahmed.problem: I spend 2 hours commuting to work every day. The buses are always late and overcrowded.

fatima.name: Fatima
fatima.problem: There's no reliable taxi service in my neighborhood. I have to walk 30 minutes to the main road.

dmitri.name: Dmitri
dmitri.problem: Deliveries take forever to arrive. Sometimes packages get lost between warehouses.

priya.name: Priya
priya.problem: My kids' school bus route is inefficient. They're on the bus for 90 minutes for a 20-minute drive.

chen.name: Chen
chen.problem: Our office building uses so much electricity. The AC runs all day even in empty rooms.

maria.name: Maria
maria.problem: Solar panels are expensive and I don't know if they're worth it for my home.

raj.name: Raj
raj.problem: The power grid in our area goes down twice a week. We lose work and food spoils.

li.name: Li
li.problem: Clean water is delivered by truck once a week. Sometimes it doesn't come and we run out.

sara.name: Sara
sara.problem: The water recycling plant is outdated. We waste 40% of our water supply.

omar.name: Omar
omar.problem: I can't reach my family back home easily. International calls are expensive and drop constantly.

yuki.name: Yuki
yuki.problem: There's no good way to share news in our community. We miss important announcements.

carlos.name: Carlos
carlos.problem: Language barriers make it hard to communicate with my neighbors from different countries.

m2.title: Dream Team
m2.description: Hire 3 team members for your startup. Balance skills, budget, and team compatibility.
m2.hint1: Your team needs three skills: development, design, and marketing.
m2.hint2: Watch out for personality conflicts. Some people don't work well together.
m2.success: Great team! You built a balanced, compatible squad within budget.
m2.fail: Your team had some issues. Try balancing roles, budget, and personality fit.

m3.title: MVP Launch
m3.description: Build your minimum viable product. Choose which features to build within a 6-week deadline.
m3.hint1: MVP means Minimum Viable Product — focus on what users NEED, not what's cool.
m3.hint2: Core features are must-haves. Nice-to-haves can wait. Risky features might backfire.
m3.success: Great launch! You prioritized the right features and customers love it.
m3.fail: Your MVP missed some key features. Focus on core functionality first.

m4.title: Investor Pitch
m4.description: Build a pitch deck, present key numbers, and convince an investor to fund your startup.
m4.hint1: A great pitch tells a story: start with the Problem, then your Solution, then show the Market opportunity.
m4.hint2: Investors love realistic numbers. A $100M market with $20K monthly revenue and a $500K ask shows you know your business.
m4.success: The investor is excited and ready to write a check!
m4.fail: The investor passed. Work on your pitch structure and numbers.

m5.title: Price It Right
m5.description: Set the right price and marketing budget. Too high = no buyers. Too low = bankruptcy.
m5.hint1: Unit economics matter: each customer should bring in more money than it costs to acquire them (CAC).
m5.hint2: Find the sweet spot where revenue exceeds costs. Don't overspend on marketing -- sustainable growth wins.
m5.success: Your business is profitable! Great pricing strategy.
m5.fail: Your business is losing money. Adjust price and marketing to find profitability.

m6.title: Crisis Mode
m6.description: A competitor copied your product. Make fast decisions to survive 5 rounds of crisis.
m6.hint1: Watch all four metrics -- if ANY one drops to zero, it's game over. Balance is key.
m6.hint2: Don't panic! Investing in product quality and team morale usually pays off more than matching competitor prices.
m6.success: Your company survived the crisis! Strong leadership under pressure.
m6.fail: Your company didn't survive. Balance all metrics -- don't let any single one collapse.

m7.title: Go Global
m7.description: Expand your startup to 5 BRICS countries. Choose entry strategies, set local pricing, and assign the right managers.
m7.hint1: Match managers to countries by language -- a Russian-speaking manager in Russia gets better results than one who only speaks English.
m7.hint2: Pricing must reflect local purchasing power. A $50 product works in Russia but not in India where average income is much lower.
m7.success: Global expansion succeeded! Your startup is now international.
m7.fail: Not enough profitable markets. Review your pricing and manager assignments.

m8.title: Data-Driven
m8.description: Analyze customer data to find the most valuable segment and allocate your marketing budget wisely.
m8.hint1: Use filters to isolate specific countries and plan types. Look at total revenue, not just number of customers.
m8.hint2: The most valuable segment has both high monthly payments AND long retention. Try filtering by Premium plan first.
m8.success: Excellent analysis! You found the key insight and allocated budget wisely.
m8.fail: The data tells a different story. Try using more filters and chart combinations.

m9.title: Exit Strategy
m9.description: A corporation offers $2.5M for your startup. Build 5-year projections, calculate NPV, and decide: sell or keep?
m9.hint1: NPV (Net Present Value) discounts future money to today's value. $1M in 5 years is worth less than $1M today.
m9.hint2: Both SELL and KEEP can be correct -- but your supporting arguments must match your financial data. Don't argue 'we'll be worth more' if your NPV is below the offer.
m9.success: Strong financial reasoning! Your decision is well-supported by the data.
m9.fail: Your arguments don't match the financial data. Review the NPV vs offer comparison.

m10.title: BRICS City Ecosystem
m10.description: Build a startup ecosystem: select 6 startups, place them in city zones, create partnerships, and distribute $1M fund.
m10.hint1: Diversity is key -- choose startups from different industries so they complement each other rather than compete.
m10.hint2: Place startups in matching zones (tech companies in Tech Hub, food startups near Residential). Connect startups that share technology tags for synergy bonuses.
m10.success: Your ecosystem is thriving! Great portfolio strategy and partnerships.
m10.fail: The ecosystem needs more synergy. Try matching startups to zones and creating partnerships between companies with shared technologies.

dashboard.title: My Career Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Based on your mission performance
dashboard.playMoreToUnlock: Complete {count} more role(s) to unlock your career profile
dashboard.professionQuestion: Which career would you like to explore further?
dashboard.independenceNoData: Complete new missions to start tracking
dashboard.noAchievements: Complete mission blocks to earn achievements.
dashboard.unlockCareerHint: Complete missions in all 3 roles to unlock your career profile
dashboard.coopBannerDesc: Your partners are waiting — click to continue
dashboard.teamworkCareerDesc: Your teamwork skill from co-op missions directly affects recommended professions. Keep playing co-op to unlock team-oriented careers!

coop.title: Cooperative Missions
coop.subtitle: Team up with engineers from BRICS+ countries
coop.turnCompleted: Your part is done! Waiting for partner.
coop.createFirst: Create a session and invite a partner
coop.asyncNote: No need to be online at the same time — take turns at your own pace
coop.partnerInactive: Your partner has been inactive for 48h
coop.partnerInactiveWarning: {count} player(s) haven't completed their mission in 48 hours
coop.replaceInactiveDesc: You can kick inactive players and reopen the session for new teammates. Kicked players won't receive any rewards.
coop.infoSubtitle: Co-op unlocks exclusive rewards and skills unavailable in solo mode
coop.b1Desc: Every co-op mission gives bonus rewards on top of your regular earnings
coop.b2Desc: The only way to level up your Teamwork competency in your profile
coop.b3Desc: 3 missions you can't complete solo — only possible together
coop.b4Desc: Play with participants from Russia, India, China, Brazil, UAE and more
coop.s1Desc: Choose a mission type, pick your role, and wait for a partner
coop.s2Desc: You solve your part, your partner solves theirs. Results combine together
coop.s3Desc: No need to be online at the same time — take turns at any hour
coop.cityDesc: Automate city infrastructure: delivery drones and robot street cleaners
coop.farmDesc: Build a smart farm: harvest robots and automated supply chains
coop.startupDesc: Launch a tech startup: entrepreneur's business plan + drone programmer's tech
coop.teamNotReadyDesc: Waiting for {needed} more player(s) to join before the mission can begin.
coop.asyncReminder: No need to be online at the same time — play when it's convenient
coop.missionDescDefault: Each player completes their role-specific task. Results combine into a team score.
coop.missionDesc_city_infrastructure: Automate city infrastructure: delivery drones and robot street cleaners working together.
coop.missionDesc_smart_farm: Build a smart farm: harvest robots and automated supply chains.
coop.missionDesc_startup_launch: Launch a tech startup: business plan + drone technology.
coop.missionDesc_coop_solar_farm: Build a solar farm: scan terrain, configure robots, and set energy prices.
coop.missionDesc_coop_bridge: Engineer a bridge: calculate load, assemble structure, and plan logistics.
coop.missionDesc_coop_rescue: Lead a rescue operation: aerial recon, ground robots, and resource management.
coop.missionDesc_coop_smart_district: Design a smart district: drone monitoring, robot maintenance, and business planning.
coop.missionDesc_coop_city_launch: Launch a new city: infrastructure scanning, construction robots, and startup ecosystem.
coop.oneSessionLimit: You already have an active session. Complete or leave it before creating a new one.
coop.oneSessionLimitHint: You already have an active session. Complete or leave it first.
coop.finishSessionHint: End the session now and calculate results based on current scores
coop.canImprove: You can replay to improve your score!
coop.startYourPart: Ready to start your part of the mission?

coopMissions.selectSubtitle: 3-player async missions. Each role does their part independently.

solarFarm.title: Solar Farm
solarFarm.desc: Build a solar farm: scan terrain, configure robots, and set energy prices.

drone.title: Terrain Scanner
drone.desc: Scan the 6x6 grid and find the 3 flattest cells (lowest elevation) for solar panel installation.
drone.submitted: Scan complete! Results sent to the team.

robot.title: Robot Configurator
robot.desc: Select the best chassis type for the terrain and configure how many solar panels (20kg each) the robot can carry.
robot.overweight: Too heavy! Reduce panel count or choose a stronger chassis.
robot.submitted: Configuration saved! Sent to the team.

entrepreneur.title: Pricing Strategy
entrepreneur.desc: Given installation costs and energy output, calculate the price per kWh to be profitable.

bridge.title: The Bridge
bridge.desc: Survey a gorge, configure a welding robot, and plan the construction budget.

drone.title: Gorge Survey
drone.desc: Fly your drone across the gorge. Measure the width, depth, and assess wind conditions.
drone.submitted: Survey data sent to the team!

robot.title: Welding Robot Setup
robot.desc: Configure the welding arm reach and select the best construction material.
robot.submitted: Robot configured! Sent to the team.

entrepreneur.title: Budget Plan
entrepreneur.desc: Create a construction budget plan. Allocate $500K across three expense categories.

rescue.title: Rescue Operation
rescue.desc: Find survivors, deploy rescue robots, and manage the emergency budget.

drone.title: Survivor Search
drone.desc: Scan the 8x8 disaster zone to find 5 survivors using heat signatures. You have 15 scans.

robot.title: Rescue Robot Deployment
robot.desc: Assign the right type of rescue robot to each survivor based on their hazard type.

entrepreneur.title: Emergency Budget
entrepreneur.desc: Allocate $100K rescue budget across 5 survivors and choose a press communication strategy.
entrepreneur.press1: Be transparent: share facts, rescue progress, and challenges honestly
entrepreneur.press2: Focus on heroism: highlight the bravery of the rescue teams
entrepreneur.press3: Minimize: downplay the situation to avoid public panic

smartDistrict.title: Smart District
smartDistrict.desc: Set up traffic monitoring, deploy service robots, and manage district operations.

drone.title: Traffic Camera Setup
drone.desc: Place 4 cameras on the grid to maximize road coverage. Roads run at rows 2, 5 and columns 2, 5.

robot.title: Service Robot Fleet
robot.desc: Select 3 service robots from a catalog of 6 and assign each to a maintenance zone.

entrepreneur.title: District Management
entrepreneur.desc: Set performance KPIs and allocate the monthly $50K operating budget.

cityLaunch.title: City Launch
cityLaunch.desc: Configure a drone fleet, design robot schedules, and plan the city launch budget.

drone.title: Drone Fleet Commander
drone.desc: Assign 5 patrol drones to city zones. Maximize zone coverage by spreading drones across all areas.

robot.title: Robot Scheduler
robot.desc: Assign 4 maintenance robots to 6 tasks. Choose the right robot for each task and avoid scheduling conflicts.

entrepreneur.title: Launch Plan
entrepreneur.desc: Allocate $500K across infrastructure, marketing, and reserves. Set key milestones.

profile.chooseAccessory: Choose an accessory for your robot
profile.usernameTooShort: Username must be at least 2 characters

certificate.desc: Receive an official RoboJunior certificate after completing all missions in at least one role.
certificate.benefit1: Digital certificate with your name and achievements
certificate.benefit2: Shareable on social media and in your portfolio
certificate.benefit3: Proof of skills for school applications and competitions

deleteAccount.title: Delete Account
deleteAccount.warning: Deleting your account is permanent. All your progress, achievements, coins, and data will be lost forever. This action cannot be undone.
deleteAccount.confirmMessage: Are you sure? This action cannot be undone. All your progress, achievements, and data will be permanently deleted.

verify.title: Verify Your Account
verify.subtitle: Get verified and unlock exclusive features

benefits.trust: More trust from other players and parents

verify.requirementsDescription: Upload a photo of a school ID or a parent confirmation email to verify your account.
verify.modalMessage: Thank you for your interest! This feature will be added in the release version of the game.

certificate.pageSubtitle: Prove your skills with an official RoboJunior certificate
certificate.whatIsDesc: The RoboJunior Completion Certificate is an official digital document confirming that you have successfully completed all missions in at least one engineering role. It includes your name, achievements, competency scores, and the date of completion.

benefits.portfolio: A professional digital certificate to add to your portfolio
benefits.social: Shareable on social media with a unique verification link
benefits.competitions: Proof of skills for school applications and STEM competitions
benefits.employers: Show future employers your engineering and teamwork abilities

certificate.howToGetDesc: Complete all 10 missions in at least one role (Drone Programmer, Robot Constructor, or Entrepreneur). The more roles you complete, the more impressive your certificate will be!

tos.title: Terms of Service
tos.serviceContent: RoboJunior is a free educational game where you learn about robotics, programming, and entrepreneurship by completing fun missions. It is made for students aged 13-18 from BRICS+ countries.
tos.ageContent:
You must be at least 13 years old to create an account.
If you are under 13, a parent or guardian must create the account for you and supervise your use of the game.
tos.accountContent:
You are responsible for keeping your password safe.
Do not share your login with anyone.
All information you provide must be truthful.
tos.behaviorContent:
Be respectful to other players at all times.
Do not bully, harass, or use offensive language.
Do not share personal information (real name, address, phone number, school) in the game.
Do not cheat or try to exploit bugs.
tos.currencyContent:
Coins and XP earned in the game have no real-world monetary value.
Virtual currency cannot be exchanged for real money.
Purchases made with in-game coins are final and non-refundable.
tos.coopContent:
When playing cooperatively with other players:
- Be a good teammate and communicate respectfully.
- Do not intentionally sabotage your team's progress.
- Report any inappropriate behavior to us.
tos.ipContent:
All game content (graphics, text, missions, code) belongs to the RoboJunior team.
You may not copy, distribute, or sell any part of the game without permission.
tos.terminationContent:
We may suspend or delete your account if you break these rules.
If your account is suspended, you may lose access to your progress and virtual items.
tos.disclaimerContent:
RoboJunior is an educational game, not a professional training program.
Career suggestions in the game are for fun and exploration only - they are not real career advice.
We do our best to keep the game running, but we cannot guarantee it will always be available.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.collectContent:
When you sign up, we collect:
- Email address
- Username (you choose this)
- Country
- Age (to verify you meet the age requirement)

As you play, we also collect:
- Game progress (missions completed, scores, stars)
- Competency scores
- Avatar customization choices
privacy.useContent:
We use your information to:
- Let you play the game and save your progress
- Match you with teammates for cooperative missions
- Show leaderboards
- Improve the game experience
privacy.storageContent:
Your data is stored securely using Supabase cloud infrastructure.
We use industry-standard security measures to protect your information.
privacy.childrenContent:
We take children's privacy seriously.
We follow the principles of COPPA (Children's Online Privacy Protection Act) and GDPR for children.
We do not knowingly collect personal information from children under 13 without parental consent.
We collect only the minimum data needed to run the game.
privacy.parentalContent:
If your child uses RoboJunior, you have the right to:
- See what data we have about your child
- Request that we delete your child's data
- Ask us to stop collecting data about your child

Contact us at robojunior@example.com to exercise these rights.
privacy.sharingContent:
We do NOT sell your personal information to anyone.
We do NOT share your data with advertisers.
We may share anonymized (non-personal) statistics to improve the game.
privacy.cookiesContent:
We use minimal cookies, only for:
- Keeping you logged in (session cookies)
- Remembering your language preference

We do not use tracking or advertising cookies.
privacy.retentionTitle: How Long We Keep Your Data
privacy.retentionContent:
We keep your data as long as your account is active.
If you delete your account, we will delete your data within 30 days.
Some anonymized game statistics may be kept for research purposes.
privacy.deletionContent:
You can request to delete your account at any time by contacting us.
When your account is deleted, all your personal data, game progress, and virtual items will be permanently removed.
privacy.contactContent:
If you have questions about your privacy or want to exercise your rights, contact us at:
Email: robojunior@example.com

missionShell.noHintsYet: Click below to reveal your first hint.

onboarding.drone_step1_desc: You will program a drone to fly across the grid. Drag command blocks to build your program.
onboarding.drone_step2_desc: Use Forward, Turn Left, and Turn Right blocks. The drone follows your commands in order.
onboarding.drone_step3_desc: Press Run to see your drone fly. If it does not reach the target, adjust your program and try again!
onboarding.robot_step1_desc: You will assemble a robot from parts. Drag components into the blueprint slots.
onboarding.robot_step2_desc: Each part costs coins. Stay within budget while meeting the mission requirements.
onboarding.robot_step3_desc: Press Test to see if your robot can complete the task. Adjust parts and try again if needed.
onboarding.entrepreneur_step1_desc: You will explore the city, talk to people, and discover problems worth solving.
onboarding.entrepreneur_step2_desc: Click on characters to hear their problems. Each problem becomes a sticky note.
onboarding.entrepreneur_step3_desc: Drag sticky notes into columns to group similar problems. Choose the best column as your startup idea!

knowledgeBase.programming_content: Programming means giving a computer step-by-step instructions. In this mission, you program a drone by arranging command blocks.
knowledgeBase.algorithms_content: An algorithm is a sequence of steps to solve a problem. The shorter and clearer your algorithm, the better!
knowledgeBase.loops_content: A loop repeats a set of commands multiple times. Use the Repeat block to avoid writing the same commands over and over.
knowledgeBase.engineering_content: Engineers design solutions that meet requirements within constraints like budget and weight.
knowledgeBase.prototyping_content: A prototype is a first version you build to test your idea. Test early, learn fast, and improve.
knowledgeBase.entrepreneurship_content: Entrepreneurs find problems and create businesses to solve them. The best ideas come from talking to real people.
knowledgeBase.market_research_content: Market research means talking to potential customers to understand their needs before building a product.
knowledgeBase.drone_commands_content:
takeoff() — launch the drone
forward(n) — move forward n cells
turnLeft() — rotate 90° left
turnRight() — rotate 90° right
land() — land the drone
flyTo(x, y) — fly to grid position
scanHeat() — detect heat signatures
dropMarker(color) — mark a position
knowledgeBase.drone_loops_content:
for (let i = 0; i < n; i++) '{' ... '}' — repeat n times
while (condition) '{' ... '}' — repeat while true
repeat(n) '{' ... '}' — block-based repeat (Easy missions)
knowledgeBase.drone_conditions_content:
if (drone.seeWall('front')) '{'
  drone.turnLeft();
'}' else '{'
  drone.forward(1);
'}'

Check walls: seeWall('front'), seeWall('left'), seeWall('right')
Check position: atExit(), atDestination(target)
knowledgeBase.drone_arrays_content:
drones[0], drones[1], drones[2] — access drone by index
targets[i] — get target position
for (let i = 0; i < drones.length; i++) '{' ... '}'
fleet.filter(d => d.battery > 30) — filter drones
fleet.sort((a,b) => a.x - b.x) — sort drones
knowledgeBase.robot_physics_content:
Mass — how heavy the robot is (kg)
Center of gravity — balance point
Torque — rotational force (Nm)
Force = Torque × Gear Ratio
Pressure = Depth × Density × Gravity
knowledgeBase.robot_materials_content:
Steel — strong, heavy, cheap
Aluminum — medium strength, light, moderate cost
Titanium — very strong, light, expensive
Composite — strongest, lightest, most expensive

Choose based on budget and requirements.
knowledgeBase.robot_gears_content:
Higher gear ratio = more force, less speed
Lower gear ratio = less force, more speed

For climbing hills: use higher ratio (more torque)
For flat surfaces: use lower ratio (more speed)
Sweet spot: balance force and speed for the task.
knowledgeBase.robot_energy_content:
Power consumption in Watts (W)
Battery capacity in Watt-hours (Wh)
Runtime = Capacity / Consumption

Example: 500Wh battery, 100W consumption = 5 hours
Solar panels add energy during daylight hours.
knowledgeBase.robot_testing_content:
The test simulation checks:
1. Can the robot complete the task?
2. Does it stay within physical limits?
3. Is the budget respected?

Smooth test = best score
Jerky test = partial score
Failed test = no physics points
knowledgeBase.entrepreneur_mvp_content:
Build the smallest product that solves the core problem.
Focus on must-have features first.
Launch fast, learn from users, iterate.

Avoid: building everything at once
Avoid: adding 'nice-to-have' features before core is solid
knowledgeBase.entrepreneur_unit_economics_content:
Revenue = Price × Customers
Profit = Revenue - Costs
CAC = Marketing Spend / New Customers
LTV = Avg Revenue Per Customer × Months Active

Healthy business: LTV > CAC (you earn more than you spend to acquire)
knowledgeBase.entrepreneur_team_content:
Cover all key roles: Development, Design, Marketing
Watch for personality conflicts
Balance skills vs. budget
A great team > a great idea

Common mistake: hiring only developers and forgetting marketing
knowledgeBase.entrepreneur_pricing_content:
Too high = nobody buys
Too low = you lose money
Sweet spot: where profit is maximized

Test different prices
Watch the profit graph, not just revenue
Consider: competitors, customer income, perceived value
knowledgeBase.entrepreneur_fundraising_content:
Pitch structure: Problem → Solution → Market → Business Model → Team → Ask
Know your numbers: TAM, SAM, SOM
TAM = Total Addressable Market (everyone who could buy)
SAM = Serviceable Market (your realistic reach)
SOM = Your actual target in year 1

Investors want: big market + strong team + traction

common.tryHarder: Try a harder difficulty for more rewards

blockEditor.emptyHint: Click blocks above to build your program...

droneMission.comingSoonDesc: This mission is under development. Try missions 1-6 to practice your skills!

droneMission1.title: First Flight
droneMission1.briefDesc: Program a drone to fly from start to the flag. Use visual blocks to create your flight path.
droneMission1.description: Your first drone mission! Program the drone to fly from the bottom-left corner to the flag at the top-right. Use takeoff, forward, turn, and land blocks.
droneMission1.tip: Hint: The drone starts facing North. Use takeoff first, then forward and turns to reach the flag. Land when you arrive!
droneMission1.success: The drone reached the target! Great job!
droneMission1.didNotReach: The drone didn't reach the flag. Try a different program!
droneMission1.offGrid: The drone went off the grid! Adjust your program.

droneMission2.title: Mapping
droneMission2.briefDesc: Fly over all 5 photo points to photograph the territory. Use repeat blocks for efficiency.
droneMission2.description: Photograph the territory by flying over all marked photo points. The fog clears as you fly. Use repeat(N) blocks to keep your program short.
droneMission2.tip: Hint: Look for patterns in the photo point layout. Using repeat(N) can save you blocks!
droneMission2.success: All photo points captured! Great mapping work!
droneMission2.partialSuccess: You photographed {visited} of {total} points. Try to visit them all!
droneMission2.offGrid: The drone went off the grid! Adjust your program.

droneMission3.title: Night Watch
droneMission3.briefDesc: Program a patrol drone for the night construction site. Cover at least 80% of the territory.
droneMission3.description: The construction site needs night surveillance. Program a patrol route that covers at least 80% of the grid. Your program runs for 3 patrol cycles.
droneMission3.tip: Hint: A snake-like pattern (forward, turn, forward, turn) covers the most ground. Use repeat to loop the pattern!
droneMission3.success: Patrol complete! Coverage: {coverage}%. The site is secure!
droneMission3.insufficientCoverage: Only {coverage}% covered. You need at least 80%. Try a different patrol pattern!
droneMission3.offGrid: The drone went off the grid! Adjust your patrol route.

droneMission4.title: Storm Delivery
droneMission4.briefDesc: Deliver medicine through a sandstorm. Wind pushes your drone after every move!
droneMission4.description: A sandstorm is raging! Wind changes direction each turn (N, E, S, W cycle). After each move, wind pushes the drone 1 cell. Compensate to reach the target!
droneMission4.tip: Hint: Wind cycles N, E, S, W. After each forward move, wind pushes 1 cell. Plan your route to compensate!
droneMission4.success: Medicine delivered despite the storm! Great piloting!
droneMission4.didNotReach: The drone didn't reach the hospital. Compensate for wind and try again!
droneMission4.offGrid: The drone went off the grid! Watch the wind direction.
droneMission4.blownOffGrid: Wind blew the drone off the grid! Compensate for the wind direction.

droneMission5.title: Drone Swarm
droneMission5.briefDesc: Write JavaScript to control 3 drones and install solar panels at 3 locations.
droneMission5.description: Your first code mission! Control 3 drones using JavaScript. Use arrays and loops to send each drone to its target and install solar panels.
droneMission5.tip: Use a for loop to control all 3 drones efficiently: for (let i = 0; i < 3; i++) '{' ... '}'
droneMission5.success: All solar panels installed! Great coding!
droneMission5.partialSuccess: Only {installed} of {total} panels installed. Check your code!

droneMission6.title: Sensor Maze
droneMission6.briefDesc: Write a wall-following algorithm to navigate a maze using sensor API.
droneMission6.description: Navigate a 10x10 maze using sensor API. The drone can see walls in front, left, and right. Write a wall-following algorithm to reach the exit with minimal wall hits!
droneMission6.tip: Try the right-hand rule: if no wall on right, turn right and go forward. If wall ahead, turn left.
droneMission6.success: Maze completed in {steps} steps with {wallHits} wall hits!
droneMission6.didNotReach: The drone didn't reach the exit. Check your algorithm!
droneMission6.tooManyHits: Reached exit but with {wallHits} wall hits (max 2). Improve your sensor usage!

droneMission7.title: Search & Rescue
droneMission7.tip: Use nested for loops to scan the entire grid. Check heat readings — 100 means survivor found!
droneMission7.notEnough: Only found {found} survivors. Need at least {needed}. Scan more of the grid!

droneMission8.title: Blind Navigation
droneMission8.tip: Use drone.camera.scan() to detect nearby landmarks, then navigate toward the target at (7, 7).
droneMission8.didNotReach: The drone didn't reach the target. Use landmarks to orient yourself!

droneMission9.title: Air Convoy
droneMission9.tip: Loop through orders, find the closest drone with findClosest(), then flyTo pickup, grab, flyTo dropoff, drop.
droneMission9.notEnough: Only delivered {delivered} orders. Need at least {needed}. Optimize your routing!

droneMission10.title: Smart City System
droneMission10.tip: Sort events by priority first! Emergencies (type === 'emergency') should be handled before deliveries and patrols.
droneMission10.notEnough: Only handled {handled} events. Need at least {needed}. Check your priority handling!

drone.m1_easy_desc: Simple delivery route — great for beginners!
drone.m1_med_desc: Program a drone to deliver medical supplies to an oil platform. Avoid obstacles.
drone.m1_hard_desc: Multiple drones, dynamic obstacles, and tight battery limits. For expert programmers.
drone.m2_easy_desc: Basic city grid navigation — learn the patterns.
drone.m2_med_desc: Optimize a drone delivery route across Riyadh to minimize time and battery use.
drone.m2_hard_desc: Rush hour in Riyadh — 7 packages, moving obstacles, 60% battery limit.
drone.addBlocksHint: Click blocks above to build your program...
drone.tip: Pick up the package, navigate to the target and drop it. Fewer blocks = higher score!
drone.crashed: Crashed into obstacle! Reset and try again.

robot.m1_easy_desc: Build a basic robot for simple pipeline tasks.
robot.m1_med_desc: Build a robot to work on an oil pipeline assembly line within a tight budget.
robot.m1_hard_desc: Critical pipeline repair — extremely tight budget, maximum stats required.
robot.m2_easy_desc: Design a simple search robot for open terrain.
robot.m2_med_desc: Design a robot for search-and-rescue operations in a desert environment.
robot.m2_hard_desc: Night rescue in collapsed ruins — all stats must be maxed within budget.

entrepreneur.m1_easy_desc: Simple startup decisions — learn the basics of entrepreneurship.
entrepreneur.m1_med_desc: Launch an educational technology startup for Saudi schools. Choose your team and strategy.
entrepreneur.m1_hard_desc: High-stakes launch — investor pressure, regulatory hurdles, 4 critical decisions.
entrepreneur.m2_med_desc: Your startup has traction. Decide whether to scale locally or expand to BRICS+ markets.
entrepreneur.m2_hard_desc: Crisis mode — competitor threat, talent flight, and expansion decision all at once.
entrepreneur.tip: There is no single right answer — your choices reflect your leadership style.
entrepreneur.m1s1_context: You are launching a tech startup in a BRICS city. Which market will you target?
entrepreneur.m1s1_fb1: Saudi Vision 2030 is driving rapid tech adoption. Great growth potential!
entrepreneur.m1s1_fb2: Strong engineering talent and growing startup ecosystem. Solid choice!
entrepreneur.m1s1_fb3: Enormous market. Scale wins here, but margins are tight.
entrepreneur.m1s2_context: Your MVP is ready. How will you fund growth?
entrepreneur.m1s2_fb1: Smart money brings mentorship and connections. Worth the equity.
entrepreneur.m1s2_fb2: Debt keeps you in control but adds monthly pressure.
entrepreneur.m1s2_fb3: Hardest path but you keep 100% ownership. Discipline wins.
entrepreneur.m1s3_context: Business is growing. Time to hire. What team structure?
entrepreneur.m1s3_fb1: Quality over quantity. High output but high burn rate.
entrepreneur.m1s3_fb2: More hands on deck. Lower cost per head but needs strong management.
entrepreneur.m1s3_fb3: Access to global talent and time zones. Communication is the challenge.
entrepreneur.m1s4_context: A major competitor just launched a free alternative. Your paid users are leaving. What now?
entrepreneur.m1s4_opt1: Go premium — double down on enterprise features
entrepreneur.m1s4_opt2: Pivot to B2B — sell to schools directly
entrepreneur.m1s4_fb1: Enterprise customers pay more and churn less. Smart positioning.
entrepreneur.m1s4_fb2: B2B contracts provide stable revenue. Schools need specialized tools.
entrepreneur.m1s4_fb3: Risky but could recapture market share fast.
entrepreneur.m2s1_context: Your startup hit $50K monthly revenue. Time to expand. Where?
entrepreneur.m2s1_fb1: Familiar territory. Lower risk, faster time to market.
entrepreneur.m2s1_fb2: Booming tech ecosystem. High competition but massive opportunity.
entrepreneur.m2s1_fb3: First-mover advantage in many sectors. Infrastructure is the challenge.
entrepreneur.m2s2_context: The new market needs a localized product. What will you build?
entrepreneur.m2s2_fb1: Physical products create moats but need supply chains.
entrepreneur.m2s2_fb2: Software scales globally with low marginal cost. Classic SaaS play.
entrepreneur.m2s2_fb3: High-touch, high-margin. Builds deep relationships but hard to scale.
entrepreneur.m2s3_context: You need a country manager for the new market. Who do you hire?
entrepreneur.m2s3_opt1: Local executive — knows the market deeply
entrepreneur.m2s3_opt2: Internal transfer — knows your culture and product
entrepreneur.m2s3_opt3: Fresh MBA — hungry, data-driven, lower cost
entrepreneur.m2s3_fb1: Local knowledge is invaluable. They speak the language of the market.
entrepreneur.m2s3_fb2: Culture fit matters. They can translate your vision to the new team.
entrepreneur.m2s3_fb3: Fresh perspective and energy. May need more mentoring though.
entrepreneur.m2s4_context: A well-funded competitor is copying your product and offering it for free. Your team morale is dropping.
entrepreneur.m2s4_opt1: Acquire them — if you cannot beat them, buy them
entrepreneur.m2s4_opt2: Differentiate — build features they cannot copy easily
entrepreneur.m2s4_fb1: Bold move. Removes the threat and gains their team.
entrepreneur.m2s4_fb2: Innovation wins long-term. Deep tech moats are hard to replicate.
entrepreneur.m2s4_fb3: Turning enemies into allies. Creative strategy.
entrepreneur.shock_regulation: New government regulation requires compliance within 30 days. Costs increase by 20%.
entrepreneur.shock_viral: Your product went viral! User signups tripled overnight but servers are struggling.
entrepreneur.shock_currency: Currency devaluation in your market. Import costs jump 30%.
entrepreneur.shock_talent: Your lead developer got poached by a tech giant. Key knowledge is walking out the door.

shop.title: Item Shop
shop.subtitle: Spend your earned coins to customize your robot
shop.noItems: No items available in this category

commands.title: Available Commands
commands.description: Your drone understands these commands: takeoff() to lift off, forward(N) to move N cells forward, turnLeft() and turnRight() to rotate 90 degrees, and land() to touch down. Each command executes in sequence.

physics.title: Physics Basics
physics.description: Every robot part has weight and strength. Heavier chassis can carry more load but need stronger motors. Balance is key: a top-heavy robot will tip over. Check that total weight stays under the limit.

glossary.title: Business Glossary
glossary.description: Key terms you'll encounter in your startup journey.

mvp.title: MVP (Minimum Viable Product)
mvp.description: The simplest version of your product that lets you test your idea with real users. Build only what's essential, launch fast, and learn from feedback.

rewards.unlockedDesc: You earned a new avatar skin!
rewards.achievements_brics_founder_desc: Complete all 30 missions across all roles
rewards.badgesDesc: Badges from missions and special achievements
rewards.achievements_coop_brics_united_desc: Play with a partner from another country
rewards.achievements_coop_perfect_sync_desc: All players score 800+ in one session

skinShop.title: Skin Shop
skinShop.subtitle: Spend your earned coins on avatar skins

shop.title: Character Shop
shop.subtitle: Customize your robot's look with coins you've earned

step1.title: Welcome to BRICS City!
step1.description: You've been recruited to help build a brand new city! Complete missions, earn rewards, and discover your ideal tech career.

step2.title: 3 Roles to Try
step2.description: Drone Programmer — write code to fly drones. Robot Constructor — design and build robots. Tech Entrepreneur — run a startup. Try all three!

step3.title: 10 Missions Per Role
step3.description: Each role has 10 missions: Easy, Medium, and Hard. Earn up to 3 stars per mission. No time pressure — think carefully!

step4.title: Team Coop Missions
step4.description: Join forces with other players! Each person picks a role. Complete your part and combine results for a team score.

step5.title: Earn Rewards
step5.description: Complete missions to earn XP, coins, and unlock avatar skins. Complete all Easy/Medium/Hard missions of a role for epic rewards!

step6.title: Career Dashboard
step6.description: Your dashboard tracks your skills across all roles. Discover where you excel — coding, engineering, or business. Find your ideal career path!

step7.title: Shop, Leaderboard & Profile
step7.description: Spend coins in the shop. Compete on the leaderboard. Customize your avatar with unlocked skins. Make it yours!

parentDashboard.title: Parent Dashboard
parentDashboard.subtitle: Monitor your account activity and safety
parentDashboard.accessDeniedDesc: This page is available only for accounts registered as parent accounts.
parentDashboard.noCompetencyData: No competency data yet. Complete missions to build your profile.
